expectations.text: behavioral_check: Checks `spyglass.__file__` (or `pip show spyglass-neuro` editable status) BEFORE recommending anything destructive — distinguishes Shape A (site-packages shadowing) from Shape B (editable desync after pyproject.toml / setup.cfg bump)
expectations.evidence: 'How to confirm in 30 seconds' block runs `print(spyglass.__file__)`, `m.__file__`, `pip show spyglass`, and `pip list | grep -i spyglass` to distinguish site-packages-shadowing from editable-checkout-pointing-elsewhere.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Notes that a `git pull` that bumps `setup.cfg` / `pyproject.toml` can desync a previously editable install; re-running `pip install -e .` from the checkout refreshes the editable wrapper
expectations.evidence: Fix #1: 'After git pull, you generally don't need to reinstall unless pyproject.toml / setup.cfg / entry points changed — but doing it once now will make future pulls just work.'

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does NOT recommend `pip install spyglass-neuro` from PyPI as the fix — that creates the site-packages-shadowing problem if the user already had an editable checkout
expectations.evidence: Doesn't recommend pip install from PyPI. Recommends `pip install -e .` against the checkout.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Mentions kernel restart for Jupyter when reinstalling — Python caches imports across `pip install` so the running kernel won't pick up the new module otherwise
expectations.evidence: Doesn't mention Jupyter kernel restart. Mentions clearing __pycache__ as a separate fix but doesn't surface that an already-running kernel won't pick up the new module after pip install -e .
